title: lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata
intro: Lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata, commonly known as the Serrated Lip Fern, is a variety of the Common Male Fern (Lastrea filix-mas) plant, which is a type of vascular plant belonging to the fern family (Dryopteridaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The therapeutic actions of Lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to relieve congestion and soothe irritated tissues. The bioactive constituents of this plant are primarily flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, headaches, and dizziness due to its high concentration of compounds like bergapten and psoralen. Prolonged exposure to these compounds can also cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight, leading to conditions like phytophotodermatitis. In severe cases, improper use of Lastrea filix-mas var. serratodentata can even lead to more serious health issues, including liver damage and kidney problems. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using any plant-derived remedies to avoid these potential side effects.
